# PAN-OS Best-Practices Report
**Date:** 2026-03-25
**Firewall:** PA-850 (192.168.11.16) 
**PAN-OS Version:** 10.2.3
**Generated by:** palo-expert (NemoClaw)

## Executive Summary
**Overall Security Posture:** Needs Improvement
**Key Strengths:** Established trust zones, detailed rule descriptions
**Top 3 Risk Areas:** 
1. Unused objects (24 address, 11 service objects) 
2. Overly permissive "any" rules
3. Missing session-end logging on allow rules

## Best-Practice Alignment by Category
### Segmentation & Zones
- Trust boundaries defined (Trust, Untrust, Wifi)
- DMZ, internal, and external zones in use

### Rule Hygiene
- Most rules have detailed descriptions
- Some overly permissive "any" rules present
- Tags used inconsistently 

### Logging & Visibility
- Session start logging enabled
- Missing session-end logging on allow rules

### Object Management
- Mix of address/service objects and inline definitions
- 24 unused address, 11 unused service objects

### Rule Lifecycle
- No hit-count tracking
- No automated rule age/expiry policy

### Security Profiles
- Threat prevention, antivirus, anti-spyware profiles enabled
- URL filtering not applied to allow rules

## Prioritized Action Plan
### High Priority
- Update "Internet" rule to use address/service objects instead of "any"
- Enable session-end logging on key allow rules

### Medium Priority 
- Standardize tag usage across rules
- Review/remove unused address and service objects

### Low Priority
- Implement rule hit-count tracking and aging policy
- Enable URL filtering on allow rules

## Benchmarking & Standards
- Mostly aligned with CIS Benchmarks for PAN-OS 10.2
- Deviations:
  - Missing session-end logging on allow rules
  - Unused objects not reviewed/removed
  - No rule age/expiry policy
